theme: Daily Patterns Vol.2 — combined signals + 200MA regime filter on Vol.1 stats

combined_streak_quartile.description: After 3+ same-color days, condition on yesterday's close position within yesterday's range. Tests whether candle structure adds signal on top of the streak.
combined_streak_quartile.verdict: Combining streak + close-quartile adds essentially nothing on top of the streak alone. The +2.46pp uplift on the bear side is the only meaningful signal, and even it is modest. The streak signal already captures most of what the quartile-close signal would add.

vol1_stats_split_by_200ma_regime.description: Each Vol.1 stat re-run separately for days when yesterday's close was above the 200-day SMA vs below. Tests whether the patterns are regime-dependent.

stats.interpretation: Bull-streak continuation is real ABOVE 200MA, near coin flip below.
Bounce-after-bear-streak works in both regimes — robust.
Continuation only works ABOVE 200MA. Below MA, near coin flip.
Bounce works in both regimes but stronger above MA.

key_findings.1: 200MA regime is the most important filter we've found. Above MA, NQ shows clear directional drift on streak / range-position stats. Below MA, those signals collapse to coin-flip.
key_findings.2: Range-extension stats (NR7 expansion, top-Q higher-high) are REGIME-INDEPENDENT — robust at ~84-86% in both bull and bear markets.
key_findings.3: Combined signals (streak + close-quartile) don't materially add edge. The streak alone captures the same information.
key_findings.4: Sample sizes for 'below 200MA' are 5x smaller than 'above MA' because NQ spent most of 27 years in uptrend. Below-MA stats have wider confidence intervals.
